skills.name: AI Fundamentals
skills.description: Core concepts of artificial intelligence, including differences between AI, ML, AGI, and modern AI systems like LLMs.
skills.notes: Covers what AI engineers do, how AI systems are built, and key concepts like training, inference, and evaluation. Also introduces LLMs and modern AI ecosystems.

skills.name: Pre-trained Models
skills.description: Using existing AI models to build applications without training from scratch.
skills.notes: Includes understanding model capabilities, context length, limitations, and providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Hugging Face.

skills.name: AI APIs & OpenAI Platform
skills.description: Working with APIs to integrate AI models into applications.
skills.notes: Covers chat/completions APIs, token management, pricing, prompt structuring, and handling responses in production systems.

skills.name: Prompt Engineering
skills.description: Designing effective prompts to guide AI models toward accurate and useful outputs.
skills.notes: Includes prompt patterns, few-shot learning, chain-of-thought, prompt optimization, and avoiding hallucinations.

skills.name: Embeddings & Vector Databases
skills.description: Representing text and data as vectors for semantic search and similarity-based tasks.
skills.notes: Covers embeddings, cosine similarity, vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS), and use cases like search, recommendation, and clustering.

skills.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
skills.description: Combining LLMs with external data sources to improve accuracy and reduce hallucinations.
skills.notes: Includes chunking, embedding pipelines, retrieval mechanisms, and generation steps. Also covers frameworks like LangChain and LlamaIndex.

skills.name: AI Agents
skills.description: Systems that use LLMs to perform tasks autonomously by interacting with tools and environments.
skills.notes: Includes agent architectures, tool usage, ReAct prompting, planning, and real-world use cases like automation and assistants.

skills.name: AI Safety & Ethics
skills.description: Ensuring AI systems are safe, fair, and aligned with user and societal expectations.
skills.notes: Covers bias, hallucinations, prompt injection attacks, privacy concerns, and best practices for building responsible AI systems.

skills.name: Open Source AI Ecosystem
skills.description: Working with open-source models and tools for building customizable AI solutions.
skills.notes: Includes Hugging Face models, Ollama, open-source LLMs, and running models locally or on custom infrastructure.

skills.name: Multimodal AI & Applications
skills.description: Building AI systems that handle multiple data types such as text, images, audio, and video.
skills.notes: Includes image generation, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, vision models, and multimodal APIs like Whisper and DALL·E.
